records.raw_product_name: SkinCeuticals C E Ferulic
positives_detail: Formulation contains 15% L-ascorbic acid at a skin-penetrating pH of 2.5-3.0.
Includes 1% vitamin E and 0.5% ferulic acid to provide 8× greater photoprotection.
Demonstrated the least oxidation after 90 days in stability testing.
negatives_detail: At $182 for a 1 oz bottle, it’s the most expensive option tested.

records.raw_product_name: Timeless 20% Vitamin C + E Ferulic Acid
positives_detail: Contains 20% L-ascorbic acid with 1% vitamin E and 1% ferulic acid for high efficacy.
Priced at roughly $25 per bottle, 85% less expensive than premium options.
Showed comparable oxidation stability at 60 days in testing.
negatives_detail: Showed slightly more yellowing than SkinCeuticals by day 90, indicating faster oxidation.
Higher 20% concentration may cause more irritation for sensitive skin users.

records.raw_product_name: Geek & Gorgeous C-Glow
positives_detail: Formulation uses 15% L-ascorbic acid at a tolerable pH of 3.0.
Contains ethyl ascorbic acid as a stabilizer for improved shelf life.
Demonstrated excellent stability over 90 days in stability testing.
Caused minimal irritation even on reactive skin types.

records.raw_product_name: The Ordinary Ascorbic Acid 8% + Alpha Arbutin 2%
positives_detail: Priced under $10, making it very budget-friendly.
Contains 8% L-ascorbic acid to reduce irritation risk.
Includes 2% alpha arbutin for hyperpigmentation brightening benefits.
negatives_detail: Provides less photoprotection than 15-20% formulations due to lower concentration.
Showed more oxidation by day 60 than premium options in stability testing.
